subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly defines ethics up front, anchoring the investigation
Consistently links ethics to CSR, survey data and stakeholder analysis
Keeps Balenciaga at the centre of discussion through background, stakeholder matrix and force-field
weaknesses: References to ethics occasionally become generic rather than analytically embedded
Lacks explicit integration of specific ethical theories (e.g., Kantian, utilitarian) in several sections
Depth of ethical analysis uneven (limited financial data to reinforce points)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this largely in band 4: the concept of ethics is well understood and applied with clear analytical thinking, and Balenciaga remains central. Occasional abstract or generic references prevent a band 5 rating, so a score of 4 reflects strong but not fully seamless integration.

strengths: Provides exactly three relevant supporting documents linking to the scandal
Balanced use of primary (Balenciaga apology) and secondary (The Cut article) sources
Documents are sufficiently in-depth to inform the ethical framework
weaknesses: Lacks industry reports or financial statements to broaden perspective
Depth of analysis for each document is moderate rather than rich
Could compare internal and external communications more thoroughly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Number of documents meets the 3â5 requirement (band 4), but the moderate depth and limited variety place this in band 3. A score of 3 reflects generally relevant and sufficiently in-depth documents without the range needed for band 4.

strengths: Selection of stakeholder mapping and force-field analysis is appropriate for an ethics focus
Tools chosen demonstrate relevance to the research question
weaknesses: Application is surface level: no clear rubric for weighting or positioning
Relevance is clear but the justification for each step is limited
Lacks link between each tool and specific ethical impact assessment
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While tools are appropriate (band 3 for selection), their superficial application and lack of methodological justification align with band 2. A score of 2 reflects some selection but weak application and relevance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses survey data and qualitative quotes to support analytical points
Effectively links figures (e.g., consumer intent, stakeholder perceptions) to ethical arguments
Interpretations (e.g., 80% ceased support) are compelling and coherent
weaknesses: Limited sample size discussion (reliability and generalizability not addressed)
Force-field analysis lacks quantitative justification or clear weighting
Figures would benefit from technical clarification (scale, margins of error)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and use are adequate and some analysis of the research question is present, placing this in band 3. A score of 3 reflects acceptable but intermittent analysis and evaluation rather than the thorough integration of band 4 or 5.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
Explicitly answers the research question about long-lasting damaging effects
Restates and synthesizes key findings concisely
weaknesses: Suggestions for further research are generic (lack detail on data sources or methods)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 conclusions fully align with evidence and explicitly answer the research question. A score of 3 is appropriate given the strong consistency, with minor room to deepen recommendations.

strengths: Logical structure with title page, table of contents and clear headings
Organization guides the reader through all sections coherently
weaknesses: Could improve navigability (e.g., separate lists of figures/tables)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The assessment meets the descriptor for an appropriate structure throughout (band 2). A score of 2 reflects clear organization with minor opportunities for enhanced navigability.

strengths: All required IA elements are present: title page, word count, citations, visuals, works cited
weaknesses: URLs break across lines and lack standard hyperlink formatting
Works cited list lacks consistent hanging indents and italics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While all elements are included, multiple formatting issues hinder professional presentation. These align with band 1, warranting a score of 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure with title page, TOC and clear headings guides the reader through background, methodology, analysis and conclusion, meeting expectations for appropriate organization.

All documents are clearly linked to the scandal and offer contrasting perspectives, but depth is moderate: no financial statements or industry data are used, limiting the range of ideas.  Thus they are relevant and sufficiently in-depth rather than richly varied.
All required IA elements (title page with word count, TOC, citations, visuals, works cited) are present and appropriately formatted.
Exactly three supporting documents (Balenciaga website, Instagram apology, The Cut article) are provided, satisfying the 3-5 requirement without exceeding five.

Both tools are applied correctly and related to the research question, but the discussion remains surface level (e.g., no weighting justification in stakeholder grid, subjective scoring in force-field).  Relevance is clear, yet application lacks the depth required for the top band.

Primary data from 20 students and qualitative secondary quotes are adequate but limited in breadth.  Figures are referenced and support points, showing acceptable â not extensive â data selection and use.

The essay moves beyond description by linking survey results to stakeholder reactions and force-field conclusions; however, analysis is intermittent and sometimes asserts rather than demonstrates impact, indicating âsomeâ rather than âmostly effectiveâ integration.
The research question is restated and explicitly answered (âhad long lasting damaging effectsâ), fulfilling the highest descriptor for this strand.
Two course tools (stakeholder mapping, force-field analysis) are appropriate for brand-image/ethics issues and demonstrate an adequate, though not extensive, selection.
The final section reiterates findings that the campaign caused long-lasting damage and supports this with earlier analytical points, maintaining consistency with evidence presented.

Balenciaga is kept at the centre of the study: background, survey items, stakeholder matrix and force-field all revolve around the firm.  Discussion of media influence, customer boycotts and the brandâs apology demonstrates clear analytical connection, yet depth is uneven (e.g., limited financial data), so the analysis is strong but not fully integrated throughout.

The student consistently defines ethics, links it to CSR, and returns to ethical questions when interpreting survey data, stakeholder positions and the force-field outcome.  Ethical considerations frame most arguments, but references to the concept occasionally become generic (âviolated all ethical boundariesâ) rather than analytically embedded in every section, indicating partial rather than seamless integration.

annotations.comment: Figure 6 effectively illustrates perceived longevity of ethical impacts but could be enhanced with demographic breakdown or crossâtabulation for richer insight.
The text linking Figure 6 back to stakeholder perceptions is well-argued and maintains analytical coherence.
The stakeholder matrix is well presented; however, clarify the criteria and evidence used to position each stakeholder in terms of influence and interest.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The discussion of media influence is insightful; deepen this by referencing ethical communication principles or CSR media theory.
The selection of force-field analysis is appropriate and demonstrates thoughtful use of decision-making tools in a business ethics context.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The force-field analysis lacks a clear rubric for weighting forces; develop a quantitative scoring method to justify the relative strengths of driving and restraining factors.
The key concept of âEthicsâ is clearly identified up front, anchoring the investigation effectively in IB Business Management. This provides a solid conceptual foundation for subsequent analysis.
The research question is stated succinctly and guides the entire assessment, ensuring clarity of purpose and focus on Balenciagaâs brand image.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically structured, guiding the reader through background, methodology, analysis, and conclusion with clear page references.
Consider adding a separate List of Figures or List of Tables to the TOC to improve navigability and signal the presence of multiple visual elements.
The in-text citation for the V&A source lacks author and date formatting; align it with the referencing style used in works cited for consistency and credibility.
The background effectively situates Balenciagaâs historical context and marketing approach, lending depth to the organizational analysis.
The definition of ethics is accurate but remains abstract; integrate a CSR or stakeholder ethics framework to ground it in the context of luxury branding.
The rationale for the research question is well articulated, linking industry expectations and stakeholder concerns to Balenciagaâs ethical standing.
A sample of 20 respondents limits the reliability and generalizability of findings; discuss how sample size and selection may affect the validity of conclusions.
The choice of stakeholder analysis and forceâfield analysis is appropriate; strengthen your rationale by linking each tool explicitly to assessing ethical impact.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Including additional industry reports or financial statements could deepen document relevance and broaden the perspective beyond media and PR sources.
Figure 2 presents survey data clearly, but explain the scale and any margins of error to enhance the technical rigour of your analysis.
The interpretation linking 55% of respondents to consumer decisionâmaking effectively supports your ethical analysis of branding.
Figure 3 shows significant consumer behaviour shifts, but outline your sampling method to demonstrate survey validity and avoid potential bias.
The note that 80% have ceased support is compelling and directly illustrates the ethical repercussions of Balenciagaâs campaign.
Figure 5 powerfully demonstrates consumer intent to abandon Balenciaga; this primary data reinforces the connection between ethics and brand loyalty.
When discussing Figure 5, integrate an ethical theory (e.g., Kantian, utilitarian) to explain why consumers react strongly to marketing ethics.
The works cited demonstrate a balanced range of primary and secondary sources, enriching your investigation with multiple perspectives.
Comparing the Instagram apology language with external media commentary would deepen understanding of internal versus external stakeholder perceptions.
The internal perspective from Balenciagaâs apology is well analyzed and effectively linked to your ethical framework.
The conclusion clearly restates the research question and delivers a concise answer, maintaining consistency with the evidence presented.
The suggestion to examine campaigns post-2022 is promising; specify which data sources or analytical lenses would yield the greatest insight.
The concluding reference to ethics feels generic; integrate specific ethical frameworks or principles to reinforce the conceptual depth of your synthesis.
Several URLs span multiple lines in the works cited list; standardize hyperlink formatting to enhance readability and professional presentation.
The list format in the works cited lacks consistent hanging indents and italics for titles; refine formatting to align with academic conventions.
